Adjusted  Free  Cash  Flow  of  $850-950 million.Amcor  CEO  Ron  Delia  said:  “Throughout  fiscal  2023,  our  teams  did  an  excellent  job  proactively  recovering inflation and reducing costs in a highly challenging environment.

  Adjusted EBIT grew modestly in comparable constant currency terms and we returned $1.2 billion of cash to shareholders.

a6480cde-78e4-43e7-b36e-031129af9f77.label: text_chunk
a6480cde-78e4-43e7-b36e-031129af9f77.text: After delivering earnings growth of 8% in the first half, demand softened considerably and customer destocking persisted through the last two quarters of the year.

9328e774-7756-4939-b5b3-774ee0e23fc1.label: text_chunk
9328e774-7756-4939-b5b3-774ee0e23fc1.text: While  we  expect  current  market  conditions  to  continue  in  the  near-term,  we  have  visibility  to  a  number  of controllable factors we believe will support a return to solid earnings growth in the second half of fiscal 2024 and leave  us  well  placed  to  grow  at  our  long  term  trend  of  high-single  digit  rates  thereafter.

7767a89f-767f-46c1-9253-79f088067ba7.label: text_chunk
7767a89f-767f-46c1-9253-79f088067ba7.text: We  are  pricing  to compensate for inflation and we expect benefits from our cost reduction and productivity initiatives will have a favorable and sustainable impact on operating leverage. In addition, we expect the headwinds from the sale of our Russian plants and higher interest expense will be largely limited to the first half.

e610d706-1560-460d-91f7-62135703bd0f.label: text_chunk
e610d706-1560-460d-91f7-62135703bd0f.text: We remain focused on our long-term growth strategy and will continue to pursue opportunities to invest in the business,  particularly  through  innovation  and  sustainability  initiatives  in  faster  growing,  higher  value  markets.

3ef3aabd-6030-4166-a857-ed8548805568.label: text_chunk

We  will  also  continue  pursuing  value-creating  M&A  and  returning  cash  to  shareholders  through  share repurchases and a compelling and growing dividend.”

Cash Returns to Shareholders

Amcor  generates  significant  annual  cash  flow,  maintains  strong  credit  metrics,  and  is  committed  to  an  investment grade credit rating.

1fc1463f-607d-4eb1-806e-d69ee2914353.label: text_chunk
1fc1463f-607d-4eb1-806e-d69ee2914353.text:
The Company's strong annual cash flow and balance sheet provide substantial capacity to reinvest in  the  business  for  organic  growth,  pursue  acquisitions,  and  return  cash  to  shareholders  through  a  compelling  and growing dividend as well as regular share repurchases.

During fiscal 2023, the Company returned approximately $1.2 billion to shareholders through cash dividends and share repurchases in addition to completing three bolt-on acquisitions.

a52fd52d-9e6b-4005-a91e-2c6f5ecb3056.label: text_chunk
a52fd52d-9e6b-4005-a91e-2c6f5ecb3056.text:
Dividend

The Amcor Board of Directors today declared a quarterly cash dividend of 12.25 cents per share (compared with 12.0 cents per share in the same quarter last year).  Combined with the last three quarterly dividends, this increases the annual  dividend  for  fiscal  2023  to  49.0  cents  per  share.

0303b66b-6e2b-4511-91da-01345b3273ad.label: text_chunk
0303b66b-6e2b-4511-91da-01345b3273ad.text: The  quarterly  dividend  declared  today  will  be  paid  in  US dollars to holders of Amcor’s ordinary shares trading on the NYSE.  Holders of CDIs trading on the ASX will receive an unfranked dividend of 18.77 Australian cents per share, which reflects the quarterly dividend of 12.25 cents per share converted at an average AUD:USD exchange rate of 0.

Amcor repurchased approximately 41 million shares (approximately 3% of total shares issued and outstanding) during fiscal 2023 for a total cost of $431 million.Amcor expects to allocate approximately $70 million of cash towards share repurchases in fiscal 2024, as part of the program previously announced in fiscal 2023.

About Amcor

Amcor  is  a  global  leader  in  developing  and  producing  responsible  packaging  solutions  for  food,  beverage, pharmaceutical, medical, home and personal-care, and other products.

56e4082a-82e9-400b-a0e8-c956dddfb095.label: text_chunk
56e4082a-82e9-400b-a0e8-c956dddfb095.text:
Amcor works with leading companies around the world to protect their products, differentiate brands, and improve supply chains through a range of flexible and rigid packaging, specialty cartons, closures and services.

 The company is focused on making packaging that is increasingly light-weighted, recyclable and reusable, and made using an increasing amount of recycled content.

Presentation of non-GAAP information

Included  in  this  release  are  measures  of  financial  performance  that  are  not  calculated  in  accordance  with  U.S.  GAAP.

79cff31b-3082-4ecf-a524-dbe2ca1cdfa9.label: text_chunk
79cff31b-3082-4ecf-a524-dbe2ca1cdfa9.text: These  measures  include adjusted EBITDA and EBITDA (calculated as earnings before interest and tax and depreciation and amortization), adjusted EBIT and EBIT (calculated as earnings before interest and tax), adjusted net income, adjusted earnings per share, adjusted free cash flow and net debt.

05d5e949-5c8b-4896-8331-390d1eb642fa.label: text_chunk
05d5e949-5c8b-4896-8331-390d1eb642fa.text: In arriving at these non- GAAP measures, we exclude items that either have a non-recurring impact on the income statement or which, in the judgment of our management, are items that, either as a result of their nature or size, could, were they not singled out, potentially cause investors to extrapolate future performance from  an  improper  base.

e6ca1851-d7a8-4834-b3e0-2aeb9846a06d.label: text_chunk
e6ca1851-d7a8-4834-b3e0-2aeb9846a06d.text:
Note  that  while  amortization  of  acquired  intangible  assets  is  excluded  from  non-GAAP  adjusted  financial  measures,  the revenue  of  the  acquired  entities  and  all  other  expenses  unless  otherwise  stated,  are  reflected  in  our  non-GAAP  financial  performance  earnings measures. While not all inclusive, examples of these items include:

• material  restructuring  programs,

c96829db-a3d9-4be2-aa50-042ec7604b83.label: text_chunk

8a5d9efc-e781-4c88-a2ec-81d05c81f6de.label: text_chunk

3627e861-fffc-48e5-a9ec-3cff0bbf2ba9.label: text_chunk
3627e861-fffc-48e5-a9ec-3cff0bbf2ba9.text:
• impacts from hyperinflation accounting; and

• impacts related to the Russia-Ukraine conflict.

Amcor also evaluates performance on a comparable constant currency basis, which measures financial results assuming constant foreign currency exchange  rates  used  for  translation  based  on  the  average  rates  in  effect  for  the  comparable  prior  year  period.

6b2e7ab6-f294-4486-93ba-f68ad509bf30.label: text_chunk
6b2e7ab6-f294-4486-93ba-f68ad509bf30.text:
In  order  to  compute  comparable constant currency results, we multiply or divide, as appropriate, current-year U.S.

 dollar results by the current year average foreign exchange rates and then multiply or divide, as appropriate, those amounts by the prior-year average foreign exchange rates. We then adjust for other items affecting comparability.

e261a09a-9413-4400-b6ac-9f9e08f218dc.label: text_chunk
e261a09a-9413-4400-b6ac-9f9e08f218dc.text: While not all inclusive, examples of items affecting comparability include the difference between sales or earnings in the current period and  the  prior  period  related  to  acquired,  disposed,  or  ceased  operations.

74325a5f-4431-416d-bc2c-3d536160c7e3.label: text_chunk
74325a5f-4431-416d-bc2c-3d536160c7e3.text: Comparable  constant  currency  net  sales  performance  also  excludes  the impact from passing through movements in raw material costs.Management  has  used  and  uses  these  measures  internally  for  planning,  forecasting  and  evaluating  the  performance  of  the  Company’s  reporting segments and certain of the measures are used as a component of Amcor’s Board of Directors’ measurement of Amcor’s performance for incentive compensation  purposes.

28030a7a-0326-43a1-8cd7-082b6ef19188.label: text_chunk
28030a7a-0326-43a1-8cd7-082b6ef19188.text: Amcor  believes  that  these  non-GAAP  measures  are  useful  to  enable  investors  to  perform  comparisons  of  current  and historical performance of the Company. For each of these non-GAAP financial measures, a reconciliation to the most directly comparable U.S. GAAP financial measure has been provided herein. These non-GAAP financial measures should not be construed as an alternative to results determined in accordance  with  U.S.

These items include but are not limited to the impact of foreign exchange translation, restructuring program costs, asset impairments, possible gains and losses on the sale of assets, and certain tax related events.

The relationships summarized involve the financial metric EBITDA and its connections to other key financial indicators:

1. **EBITDA and Net Income**: EBITDA serves as a precursor to Net Income, playing a crucial role in its calculation by accounting for interest, taxes, and depreciation. This relationship highlights how EBITDA is foundational in understanding a company's profitability.

2. **EBITDA and EBIT**: Both EBITDA and EBIT are essential metrics for evaluating a company's profitability. However, they differ in that EBITDA includes adjustments for depreciation and amortization, while EBIT does not. This distinction is significant for financial analysis.

3. **EBITDA's Adjustments**: EBITDA is derived by adjusting Net Income for non-cash expenses such as Depreciation and Amortization. These adjustments are important as they impact taxable income but do not influence cash flow, emphasizing the cash-generating ability of a business.

Overall, these relationships illustrate the interconnectedness of EBITDA with Net Income and EBIT, as well as the importance of adjusting for non-cash expenses to accurately assess a company's financial health.

The relationships outlined involve the financial instrument known as Interest Rate Swaps and their valuation and usage in risk management. 

1. **Interest Rate Swaps and Valuation Technique**: Interest Rate Swaps are valued using the Discounted Cash Flow Method. This technique assesses their fair value by calculating expected future cash flows, which is crucial for understanding their financial impact.

2. **Interest Rate Swaps and Fair Value Hedges**: Interest Rate Swaps are commonly employed as fair value hedges. This application helps manage the risks associated with interest rate fluctuations, thereby safeguarding the value of the underlying debt.

In summary, Interest Rate Swaps are integral financial instruments that are valued through the Discounted Cash Flow Method and are primarily used to mitigate risks related to interest rate changes, ensuring the stability of debt values.

The relationships involving Forward Exchange Contracts highlight their financial significance and utility in managing currency risks. 

1. **Forward Exchange Contracts and Other Income**: Forward exchange contracts can generate other income through gains or losses that arise from fluctuations in currency values. This indicates their potential impact on financial performance, as they can contribute positively or negatively to income based on market conditions.

2. **Forward Exchange Contracts and Foreign Currency Translation**: These contracts are frequently utilized to hedge against potential losses from foreign currency translation. By doing so, they provide a protective measure against the volatility of exchange rates, ensuring that businesses can mitigate risks associated with currency fluctuations.

In summary, Forward Exchange Contracts serve a dual purpose: they can create additional income through currency fluctuations and act as a safeguard against losses in foreign currency translation, underscoring their importance in financial risk management.
